honest calculator website - peluqueria website - hair salon website - barberia website - vertical landing page - css theme class - hair theme --- # Professional-Services Website (Architecture A) Build a standalone Next.js 15 site for a professional services firm (consultancy, law firm, advisory, B2B supplier) with dual-audience routing or single-audience lead gen. **Two distinct sub-types:** | Sub-type | Example | Content Pattern | CTA Pattern | |----------|---------|----------------|-------------| | **B2C Advisory** | Golden Visa, Law Firm | Dual-path (investor/business), testimonials, comparison tables | Book a call, contact form | | **B2B Lead Gen** | Glass supplier, industrial products | Technical specs, project portfolio, certifications | "Request quote" → WhatsApp, quote form | ## When to Use This vs the Builder | Criteria | Use Standalone (Arch A) | Use Builder (Arch B) | |---|---|---| | Dual-path routing (investor vs business) | ✅ | ❌ (single flow) | | Age-gate / path-choice modal | ✅ | ❌ | | Complex comparison tables | ✅ | Limited | | FAQ with multi-tab switching | ✅ | Limited | | 5+ custom pages | ✅ | ❌ (builder does 1 page) | | Team has 3+ staff profiles | ✅ | ✅ | | Simple brochure (home + contact) | ❌ | ✅ | | B2B with technical product catalog | ✅ | ❌ | | Quote request form | ✅ | ❌ | | No prices (custom quoting) | ✅ N/A | ✅ N/A | ## Mobile-First Responsive Pattern Applied to Dayah LitWorks and Oz Montanía — two very different visual styles, same responsive fixes. Use this section as a portable checklist when making any client site mobile-friendly. ### Header: Animated SVG Hamburger + Slide Drawer Replace 3 hand-coded `` bars with an animated SVG hamburger. The drawer slides in from the right with backdrop blur and scroll lock. **Key elements:** - SVG hamburger that rotates 90° into an X on open - `translate-x-0`/`translate-x-full` slide animation (not `max-h` toggle) - `pointer-events-auto`/`pointer-events-none` for smooth backdrop transitions - `md:hidden` breakpoint for mobile (not `lg:hidden` — 5-7 links fit `md`) - Scroll lock when drawer is open via `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` - Escape key closes the drawer ### WhatsApp Float — Mobile Positioning ```tsx className="fixed bottom-20 right-4 z-50 ... md:bottom-6" ``` The `bottom-20` positions the float above the `MobileCta` sticky bar on mobile. Desktop falls back to `bottom-6`. The MobileCta bar itself uses `fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-40 md:hidden` with `pb-24` on footer. ### Responsive Font Sizes | Element | Mobile | Tablet (sm/md) | Desktop (lg+) | |---------|--------|----------------|---------------| | H1 (hero) | `text-4xl` | `text-6xl` | `text-7xl` | | H2 (section title) | `text-2xl` | `text-3xl` | `text-3xl` | | H3 (card title) | `text-base` | `text-lg` | `text-lg` | | Body | `text-sm` | `text-base` | `text-base` | | Small text / labels | `text-xs` or `text-[10px]` | `text-sm` | `text-sm` | | Stats number | `text-2xl` | `text-3xl` | `text-4xl` or `text-5xl` | ### Padding Spacing | Component | Mobile | Desktop | |-----------|--------|---------| | Section (large) | `py-12` | `py-24` or `py-20` | | Section (small) | `py-10` | `py-16` | | Hero top (with header) | `pt-24` | `pt-32` | | Card interior | `p-4` | `p-6` | | Grid gap | `gap-4` | `gap-6` or `gap-8` | ### Grid Breakpoints | Grid | Mobile | Tablet | Desktop | |------|--------|--------|---------| | 1-col sections | `grid-cols-1` | `sm:grid-cols-2` | `lg:grid-cols-3` or `4` | | Portfolio/Books | `grid-cols-2` | `md:grid-cols-3` | `lg:grid-cols-4` | | Feature cards | `grid` (single) | `sm:grid-cols-2` | `xl:grid-cols-5` | | Stats | `grid-cols-4` with `gap-3` | — | `gap-8` | ### Buttons — Full Width on Mobile ```tsx className="w-full sm:w-auto" ``` Apply to ALL primary/secondary CTAs. On mobile every call-to-action button should span the container width. ### Touch Feedback Add `active:scale-[0.98]` on all clickable elements for tactile feedback on mobile. `hover:` transforms work on desktop; on mobile they simply do nothing — `active:` provides the equivalent. ### What NOT To Do - **Don't use text-only hamburger** — always use animated SVG icons - **Don't use `lg:hidden` for 5-7 links** — `md:hidden` is the correct breakpoint - **Don't nest drawer inside header** — render it at root level as a sibling with backdrop overlay - **Don't skip scroll lock** — this is the #1 mobile UX complaint in production - **Don't use `max-h` transitions for nav** — they're janky. Use `translate-x` slide instead - **Don't forget `pb-24` on footer** when a MobileCta sticky bar exists (accounts for the fixed bar at the bottom) - **Don't forget `grid-cols-2`** for portfolio/books grids on mobile — single column wastes screen space ### 🚩 Admin Pages Importing @ai-whisperers/client-kit The template ships `app/admin/content/page.tsx` and `app/api/admin/content/route.ts` that import from the private `@ai-whisperers/*` registry. After removing the packages from `package.json`, these files throw build errors. **Fix — replace with disabled stubs:** ```tsx // app/admin/content/page.tsx export default function AdminContentPage() { return (

Panel de contenido desactivado. Editar content/es.json directamente.

) } ``` ```tsx // app/api/admin/content/route.ts import { NextResponse } from "next/server" export async function GET() { return NextResponse.json({ error: "Content API disabled." }, { status: 501 }) } export async function POST() { return NextResponse.json({ error: "Content API disabled." }, { status: 501 }) } ``` --- ## Sub-type A: B2C Advisory (Golden Visa Pattern) See the original implementation in `references/golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md`. Key traits: - Entry modal (language + path selection) - Investor and Business landing paths - Testimonials, trust signals, team profiles - Dark theme with gold accents --- ## Sub-type E (NEW — May 2026): Food-Service / Meal-Prep Landing Page from Docs Repo Build a single-page landing page for a food-delivery / meal-prep / subscription food service business, where the client already has a business documentation repo (pricing, copy, brand, ops docs) but no website. **Reference session:** May 13, 2026 — De Abasto a Casa from meal-prerp-website repo. ### When to Use - Client has a complete business docs repo (50+ markdown files: pricing, kitchen manuals, brand guidelines, copy, business model) - Service has 3+ tiered pricing levels with sub-options (individual/pair/family) - Lead capture is WhatsApp-only (no e-commerce, no cart, no signup) - Target: Paraguayan consumers, prices in Gs., "vos" informal tone - Honest marketing: "si no te conviene, te lo decimos" approach - Brand colors: natural/earthy (green, terracotta, crema) ### Workflow: Docs Repo → Website ``` Step 1: Read the docs repo completely ├── Read: Master Plan (business model, pricing, target) ├── Read: Copy ES (all website copy in markdown) ├── Read: Brand Guidelines (palette, fonts, voice rules) ├── Read: Website Plan (section ordering, page config) ├── Read: Image Curation (which photos to use, specifications) └── Read: Service Matrix (exact pricing per tier) Step 2: Structure content into content/es.json ├── Copy every section's text exactly (headlines, subheadlines, descriptions) ├── Preserve the "vos" informal Paraguayan tone ├── Structure into home.hero, home.services (with levels.plans[]), home.howItWorks, etc. ├── Set defaults for calculator inputs from actual market data in the docs └── Leave placeholder values in (whatsapp, email, testimonial authorizations) Step 3: Clean up inherited scaffold ├── Remove @ai-whisperers/* deps from package.json (NOT used by standalone landing pages) ├── Delete stale pages/ JSON configs OR rename to pages-json-config/ (Next.js 16 detects pages/ as Pages Router) ├── Delete any app/ directory outside src/ (conflicts with App Router) ├── Remove stale admin/api routes, blog posts, contact forms from template └── Run npm install with clean lockfile Step 4: Create components in parallel batches ├── Batch 1: Header (sticky, scroll blur), Hero (gradient, CTAs), Services (3-tier matrix) ├── Batch 2: HowItWorks (timeline 4 steps), Calculator (interactive 'use client'), Gallery (9-placeholder grid) ├── Batch 3: Sourcing (4 pillars), Team, Testimonials, FAQ (accordion), Contact, CTA Banner, Footer, WhatsAppFloat └── Each component receives content through direct import from @/content/es.json Step 5: Build & verify ├── npm run build (check for 0 errors) ├── Verify Static Generation: "○ (Static) prerendered as static content" └── Fix any Pages Router conflicts (rename pages/ directory) Step 6: Docker build & deploy ├── Use node:20-slim (NOT alpine — lightningcss needs glibc) ├── 3-stage Dockerfile: deps → builder → runner (npm install first, npm run build second, standalone third) ├── docker save | bzip2 | ssh | docker load (image transfer) └── docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml Step 7: Post-launch cleanup ├── Replace WhatsApp placeholder with real number (in both site.json and content/es.json) ├── Replace testimonial placeholders (get written authorization per person) ├── Upload real gallery photos (9 images, WebP < 200KB each, 4:3 ratio) ├── Replace email placeholder └── Add real logo SVG when available ``` ### Scaffold Cleanup Checklist When starting from a template that has e-commerce or multi-tenant scaffold: ```bash # 1. Remove stale @ai-whisperers deps from package.json # These ship e-commerce/admin/commerce stacks NOT used by landing pages # If landing page doesn't import any @ai-whisperers/* package, remove them all # 2. Handle pages/ directory conflict # Next.js 16 errors: "pages and app directories should be under the same folder" mv pages pages-json-config # If they're JSON configs, not Pages Router pages # 3. Remove stale app/ directory outside src/ rm -rf app/ # If this contains old admin/content/api routes # 4. Clean install rm -rf node_modules .next package-lock.json npm install ``` ### Component Architecture ``` src/components/sections/ ├── Header.tsx # 'use client' — sticky, scroll detect, mobile hamburger ├── Hero.tsx # 'use client' — gradient bg, CTAs, scroll-to sections ├── Services.tsx # Server — 3-tier pricing matrix with badges ├── HowItWorks.tsx # Server — 4-step timeline ├── Calculator.tsx # 'use client' — interactive savings calculator ├── Gallery.tsx # Server — 3x3 grid, SVG gradient placeholders ├── Sourcing.tsx # Server — 4-column trust pillars ├── Team.tsx # Server — founder profile card ├── Testimonials.tsx # Server — 3 testimonial cards ├── FAQ.tsx # 'use client' — accordion ├── Contact.tsx # Server — split layout CTA + info ├── Footer.tsx # Server — dark bg, nav links, copyright └── WhatsAppFloat.tsx # 'use client' — fixed position pulse button ``` ### Component Rules 1. **Server by default** — only 'use client' for interactive pieces (Header scroll, Calculator logic, FAQ accordion, WhatsAppFloat animation) 2. **Import content directly** — each component imports from `@/content/es.json`. Server components use default import; client components do the same (works because Next.js treats JSON modules as both server and client-safe) 3. **Tailwind v4 CSS variable syntax** — use `className="bg-[var(--color-mercado)]"` not `className="bg-mercado"` unless the token is registered in `@theme` and the class is generated 4. **Don't pass props from page.tsx to section components** — each section reads its own data. This keeps page.tsx clean and components self-contained. Only the Hero component optionally accepts overrides for flexibility ### Docker Image for node:20-slim (NOT alpine) ```dockerfile FROM node:20-slim AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./ RUN npm install FROM node:20-slim AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules COPY . . ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 RUN npm run build FROM node:20-slim AS runner WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./ COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public USER nextjs EXPOSE 3000 CMD ["node", "server.js"] ``` ### Pitfalls - **@ai-whisperers deps in scaffold**: The template pre-ships 15+ packages from `@ai-whisperers/*` registry. If the landing page doesn't import any, remove ALL of them from both `dependencies` and `devDependencies`. They cause `npm install` (and therefore Docker build) to fail because the packages require a GitHub token for the private registry. - **pages/ directory conflict**: If the repo has a `pages/` directory with JSON configs, Next.js 16 interprets it as Pages Router and errors: "pages and app directories should be under the same folder". Rename to `pages-json-config/` or `config/pages/`. - **app/ outside src/**: Some templates have both `app/` (outside src, older admin routes) and `src/app/` (App Router). Delete `app/` — it's stale admin content. Check with `find . -maxdepth 1 -name "app" -type d`. The admin and API routes inside will reference @ai-whisperers packages not installed, causing build errors. - **node:20-alpine + Turbopack**: Alpine's musl libc can't load `lightningcss.linux-x64-musl.node`. Use `node:20-slim` instead. The image size difference is ~50MB, negligible. - **Standalone prerendered HTML serving**: Next.js 16 standalone output may return `Cannot GET /` even when prerendered `index.html` exists in `.next/server/app/`. This occurs when the standalone server can't locate prerendered assets. The local `next start` works because it uses the full Next.js server; the standalone `server.js` may not. Workaround: verify locally first, and if standalone fails, use `docker exec` to check the container's `.next/server/app/` directory for the `index.html` file. - **Calculator wants JSON defaults from content**: The interactive calculator needs sensible defaults from the docs repo (gasto supermercado ~350k Gs/mes, valor hora ~30k Gs, etc.). Extract these from the docs' pricing analysis or client interview data, don't invent them. - **Testimonial section says "ilustrativo"**: Never ship testimonials as real — label them clearly as placeholders until the client provides written authorization per person. - **Docker Swarm labels must be under deploy.labels**: In `docker-compose.yml` for Swarm mode, Traefik labels MUST be at `services.web.deploy.labels`, NOT `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone reads labels from `services.web.labels`, but Swarm only reads `deploy.labels`. Labels at the wrong level will exist on the container but Traefik won't see them. Verify with: `docker service inspect _web --format '{{.Spec.Labels}}'`. - **node:20-slim has no wget**: Healthcheck commands using `wget` will silently fail. Fix: `HEALTHCHECK ... CMD node -e "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000/',r=>process.exit(r.statusCode===200?0:1)).on('error',()=>process.exit(1))"`. This also avoids BusyBox IPv6 resolution issues. - **Image transfer to VPS without registry**: When no Docker registry is available, transfer via pipe: `docker save :prod | bzip2 | ssh "bunzip2 | docker load"`. Then force service update: `docker service update --image :prod --force _web`. Verify: `docker service ps _web --no-trunc`. - **Cloudflare DNS not configurable from CLI**: The Cloudflare API token in this environment is read-only for zones. All DNS A records must be added through the Cloudflare web dashboard. Zone paragu-ai.com uses nameservers `elliot.ns.cloudflare.com` / `maria.ns.cloudflare.com`. No API-based DNS automation available. ## Sub-type B: B2B Lead Gen (Pitchy Pattern) Build a B2B site from the `Ai-Whisperers/template-nextjs-client` template. This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C (NEW — May 2026): Single-Locale App Router (Standalone Pattern) Build a clean Next.js 16 App Router site for professional services (law firms, notaries, accounting firms, consultancies) with a single Spanish locale, JSON-driven content, and NO @ai-whisperers dependencies. **Reference repos:** `Ai-Whisperers/escribania-paraguay`, `Ai-Whisperers/estudio-contable-paraguay` ### When to Use - Professional services (notary, accounting, law, consulting) - Single locale (Spanish/Paraguay) - No e-commerce, no user accounts, no database - Content-driven (all text in JSON, client doesn't need CMS) - 6-12 static pages, no dynamic routes beyond service/[slug] - Brand needs own theme (not builder's generic tokens) ### Stack | Component | Choice | |-----------|--------| | Framework | Next.js 16 + TypeScript 5 + React 19 | | Router | App Router (`app/`) — flat, no locale prefix | | Styling | Tailwind v4 (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) + CSS custom properties | | Icons | lucide-react | | Fonts | Google Fonts via `next/font/google` | | Content | JSON files in `content/` (single spanish file) | | Deploy | Docker Swarm + Traefik on VPS | | Image | `node:20-alpine`, 3-stage Dockerfile, `output: "standalone"` | | CI/CD | Manual: `npm run build && docker build && docker stack deploy` | ### Project Structure ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout + fonts + metadata + JSON-LD │ ├── page.tsx # Homepage (9+ sections) │ ├── globals.css # Tailwind v4 @theme + @layer components │ ├── sitemap.ts # Dynamic sitemap │ ├── robots.ts # Robots.txt │ ├── servicios/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Services listing │ │ └── [slug]/page.tsx # Service detail (SSG via generateStaticParams) │ ├── [pagina]/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Optional catch-all for secondary pages │ ├── contacto/page.tsx │ ├── faq/page.tsx │ ├── nosotros/page.tsx # About/team page │ ├── planes/page.tsx # Pricing plans (accounting) │ └── privacidad/page.tsx ├── components/ │ ├── header.tsx # Sticky nav, mobile hamburger ('use client') │ ├── hero.tsx # Dark/light variants, optional CTAs │ ├── footer.tsx # Multi-column from content JSON │ ├── services-preview.tsx # Homepage service cards │ ├── service-categories.tsx # Full service grid for /servicios │ ├── service-detail-hero.tsx # Dark hero for service/[slug] │ ├── service-benefits.tsx # Benefits grid on detail pages │ ├── service-items-list.tsx # Check-list of features │ ├── service-process.tsx # Process steps │ ├── stats-section.tsx # Numbered stats with accent bg │ ├── trust-section.tsx # Trust signals grid │ ├── process-section.tsx # Numbered process (homepage) │ ├── testimonials-section.tsx │ ├── faq-section.tsx # Accordion ('use client') │ ├── faq-categories.tsx # Categorized FAQ ('use client') │ ├── contact-section.tsx # Dark CTA with WhatsApp button │ ├── contact-info.tsx # Address/phone/email cards │ ├── contact-form-section.tsx # Form ('use client') │ ├── intro-section.tsx # Simple centered text │ ├── about-intro.tsx # Vision + mission (about page) │ ├── values-section.tsx # Values grid │ ├── team-section.tsx # Team member cards │ ├── pricing-section.tsx # Pricing cards (homepage) │ ├── plans-section-content.tsx # Full pricing table (/planes) │ ├── privacy-content.tsx # Privacy policy sections │ ├── cta-banner.tsx # Gradient CTA banner │ ├── whatsapp-float.tsx # Floating button ('use client') │ └── animations/scroll-reveal.tsx # Scroll animation ('use client') ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # ALL text in one file │ ├── site.json # Site name, phone, email, social, whatsapp │ ├── pages.json # Section configuration per page │ └── pages/ │ └── servicios.json # Individual service data for [slug] pages ├── public/ │ ├── favicon.svg │ └── opengraph-image.svg ├── Dockerfile # 3-stage alpine, standalone output ├── docker-compose.yml # Swarm-compatible with Traefik labels ├── next.config.ts # Standalone, security headers ├── package.json # Only: next, react, react-dom, lucide-react, tailwindcss ├── postcss.config.mjs # Minimal: @tailwindcss/postcss └── tsconfig.json # Standard Next.js with @/ alias ``` ### Content Architecture (3-Layer) ``` es.json → ALL text, organized by page: home.hero, serviciosPage.hero, etc. site.json → Metadata: name, phone, whatsapp config, social links pages.json → Section ordering per page: { "home": { "sections": [...] } } pages/servicios.json → Individual service data: escrituras, poderes, contabilidad, etc. ``` ### Component Development Rules 1. **Server by default, 'use client' only for:** - Stateful interactions (accordion, mobile menu, form submission) - Browser APIs (IntersectionObserver for scroll reveal) - Animation timers (whatsapp float pulse) 2. **Hero component pattern — optional CTAs:** ```tsx interface HeroProps { headline: string subheadline: string ctaPrimaryText?: string // optional — pages without CTAs omit these ctaPrimaryHref?: string ctaSecondaryText?: string ctaSecondaryHref?: string variant?: 'dark' | 'light' } ``` Conditionally render the CTA buttons only when props are provided. 3. **CSS variable pattern (Tailwind v4 compatible):** ```tsx className="bg-[var(--color-primary)] text-white" // NOT: className="bg-primary text-white" (Tailwind v3) ``` 4. **Theme in globals.css:** ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #1a1a2e; --color-accent: #c9a96e; --color-background: #faf9f6; --color-surface: #ffffff; --color-surface-alt: #f5f3ee; --color-muted: #6b7280; --font-body: "Inter", system-ui, sans-serif; --font-heading: "Playfair Display", Georgia, serif; } @layer components { .container-page { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 1.5rem; } .section-padding { padding: 5rem 0; } .section-padding-sm { padding: 3rem 0; } .gradient-accent { background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--color-accent), var(--color-accent-light)); } } ``` ### Service Detail Pages (SSG) Each service gets its own SSG page at `/servicios/[slug]`: ```tsx // app/servicios/[slug]/page.tsx import serviciosData from "@/content/pages/servicios.json" export function generateStaticParams() { const servicios = serviciosData as Record return Object.keys(servicios).map((slug) => ({ slug })) } export default async function ServicioDetailPage({ params }: Props) { const { slug } = await params const service = servicios[slug] if (!service) notFound() // Render: ServiceDetailHero → ServiceBenefits → ServiceItemsList → ServiceProcess → CtaBanner } ``` ### Development Cadence for New Sites 1. Scaffold from escribania pattern (copy all components + configs) 2. Replace: `globals.css` theme colors, content/es.json entirely 3. Add/remove pages (remove team/values if not needed, add pricing if needed) 4. Remove unused components 5. Update Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml (image name, domain, Traefik labels) 6. `npm install && npm run build` to verify 7. `docker build && docker stack deploy` ### Pitfalls - **Tailwind v4 class names differ from v3**: `bg-primary` becomes `bg-[var(--color-primary)]` unless the color is registered in `@theme` (which does generate `bg-primary` etc.). But when in doubt, use the CSS variable syntax directly. - **The Hero component needs optional CTAs**: Not all pages have CTA buttons. Make `ctaPrimaryText` and `ctaPrimaryHref` optional, not required. - **Service detail can't pass `icon` prop to ServiceItemsList**: The list component has no icon prop. Remove the prop from the calling code. - **Content JSON uses {{mustache}} placeholders**: The actual site content in es.json has `{{businessName}}`, `{{whatsapp}}`, `{{phone}}`, `{{email}}`, `{{year}}`, `{{whatsappMessage}}` that components must resolve at render time or replace in the JSON before deployment. - **Docker Swarm requires external network**: All stacks use `networks: { agent-net: { external: true } }`. Verify the network exists: `docker network ls | grep agent-net`. - **Reserved port 3000**: In Swarm mode, the `ports: ["3000"]` declaration publishes the port only for the Swarm ingress routing mesh, NOT the host directly. Use `docker exec` or curl through the overlay network to verify. - **content es.json has site metadata at root level**: The root object includes `siteName`, `siteTitle`, `siteDescription` alongside `navigation`, `footer`, `home.*`, etc. AND `site.json` has overlapping `site.name`, `site.businessName`, etc. Components typically read from `es.json` for display content and `site.json` for whatsapp/meta config. ## Sub-type D: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components AND reconstruct pageContent properly: ```tsx if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` **Why this matters**: Old `sections.tsx` components access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust` — they expect the key name to be the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`). Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).** If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de) — nav item hrefs, hero CTA links, hero CTA secondary links, blog-related URLs, and ANY href field. A missed multiclient path in content/en.json nav hrefs causes EVERY nav link in the EN locale to 404. The Header component must prepend the locale prefix to nav hrefs (e.g., `/servicios` → `/en/servicios`) after stripping any remaining multiclient prefix. Use a grep across all locale files: `grep -rn '/s/' content/*.json`. - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **Blog post links must include locale prefix**: Blog "Leer más" / "Read more" links in the BlogSection component must prepend the current locale. Without this, blog links on EN/NL/DE pages navigate to `/blog/post-slug` (no locale) which hits the catch-all route without the locale prefix, causing a 404 or wrong-content render. Fix: use `useRouter` from `next/router` and prepend: `href={\`/${locale}/blog/${post.slug}\`}`. The locale variable comes from the page component's `locale` prop, NOT from `router.locale` (which may be undefined in Pages Router). - **Content JSON corruption from patch tool**: The `patch` tool can corrupt large JSON files (208KB, 900+ lines) by misplacing commas. A missing comma between `}` and `\"seo\"` breaks the entire site — every page SSR returns `content: {}` with zero Docker errors. **Always validate after every JSON edit**: `python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')\"`. For safety, prefer Python manipulation: `json.load()` + `json.dump()` instead of `patch` or `sed` on large JSON files. Add pre-deploy validation: `for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$f'))\" && echo \"$f: VALID\" || exit 1; done`. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Hero CTA hrefs share the same problem**: Nav hrefs aren't the only place multiclient paths hide. Hero CTAs (`home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref`, `home.hero.ctaSecondaryHref`) in EN/NL/DE locale files also use `/s/en/nexa-paraguay/` paths. These cause the hero buttons on non-ES locales to 404. Run a systematic grep: `grep -rn 'cta.*Href' content/*.json | grep '/s/'`. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **buildPageContent removal breaks section rendering**: Removing `buildPageContent` is recommended (it's redundant once the dispatch passes sectionData directly), BUT you must reconstruct `pageContent` with the correct key name. Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero` — they expect the content key's LAST segment (`home.hero` → `hero`), NOT the section id (`hero` happens to match but `stats-counter` doesn't match `stats`). The dispatch must use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` as the pageContent key. Without this, ALL section data appears invisible despite the component rendering — the hero background image won't load, trust/programs/process images won't appear, and stats won't show values. See the "Passing Images to Section Components" section above for the correct dispatch code. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. - **Docker service update may not deploy new config**: After `docker build` and `docker service update --image`, the new container may still have old content JSON due to Docker build cache layers. Always verify inside the container: `docker exec $(docker ps --filter name= -q) cat /app/nexa-pages/home.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([s['id'] for s in d['sections']])"`. If old, force a restart: `docker service update --force `. - **Content duplication across pages is common**: Pages Router section-driven sites easily accumulate duplicate content when page configs share section IDs or content paths point to identical data. Always run a systematic scan (see `references/content-duplication-audit-pattern.md`) before deploying new pages. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "node", "-e", "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000/',r=>process.exit(r.statusCode===200?0:1)).on('error',()=>process.exit(1))"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR) with Next.js 16 Next.js 16's PPR (`experimental.ppr: true` + `cacheComponents: true`) streams dynamic content over a static HTML shell. Implemented on Nexa Paraguay. ### Requirements ```js // next.config.js const nextConfig = { experimental: { ppr: true, pprCacheComponents: true, // or: ppr: { cacheComponents: true } } } ``` ### Constraints (PPR strictly enforces these) With `cacheComponents: true`, the following are **forbidden** in page/layout exports: - `revalidate` (export or segment config) - `dynamicParams` - `dynamic` - `fetch` with `revalidate`/`cache` options - `generateStaticParams` must be `async` but `generateMetadata` must be **sync** To bypass: put uncached data inside `` children that use `use` (React 19) or `fetch`. The static shell renders immediately, dynamic content streams in. ```tsx // [locale]/page.tsx — PPR-ready pattern export const experimental_ppr = true // NO revalidate, NO dynamic, NO dynamicParams exports export async function generateStaticParams() { // returns all locale+slug combos for PPR to pre-render } // Synchronous generateMetadata required for PPR export function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: any }) { const { content } = getPageData(params.locale) return { title: content?.seo?.title, description: content?.seo?.description } } export default function Page({ params }: { params: any }) { const { content } = getPageData(params.locale) return ( <> {/* Static section — renders in HTML shell */} {/* Dynamic section — streams in */} }> ) } ``` ### Data Loading Pattern Since `generateStaticParams` must be async but `generateMetadata` must be sync, load data with a **memoized sync function** that reads from disk or cache: ```tsx // lib/page-data.ts — sync, reads from filesystem at build time import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs' import { join } from 'path' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const REPO = process.cwd() function loadPageData(locale: string, slug?: string) { // Read content JSON const content = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'content', `${locale}.json`), 'utf-8')) // Read page config JSON const pageConfig = slug && slug !== 'home' ? JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, 'nexa-pages', `${slug}.json`), 'utf-8')) : { sections: [{ id: 'home', content: 'home' }] } return { content, pageConfig } } export { loadPageData, LOCALES } ``` ### Static Shell Content Components rendered OUTSIDE `` must not use: - `useRouter()`, `useSearchParams()`, `usePathname()` — these are dynamic - `new Date()` in JSX — creates dynamic content; hardcode years - `window`, `localStorage`, `document` — SSR will throw - `headers()` or `cookies()` in page components For layout components like footer that need a year, hardcode it or use `export const dynamic = 'force-static'` on a separate component: ```tsx // Footer with static year — avoids uncached data const COPYRIGHT_YEAR = '2026' // NOT: String(new Date().getFullYear()) ``` ### JSON Data Nesting Blog posts stored as `{ "posts": [...] }` (object with array, not flat array). The `posts` key is the wrapper — access with: ```tsx const posts = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path)) const list = posts.posts || posts // handle both formats ``` ### Build Verification ```bash # Build output shows ◐ for PPR pages, ○ for static, ƒ for dynamic npx next build | grep -E '(◐|○|ƒ)' # Expected output: # ◐ /[locale] Partial Prerender # ◐ /[locale]/[slug] Partial Prerender # ◐ /[locale]/blog/[slug] Partial Prerender # ○ /admin Static # ƒ /api/contact Dynamic ``` ### Pitfalls - **PPR + `useRouter` conflict**: Client components inside PPR pages can use `useRouter`, but if they're outside Suspense boundaries, they can break static shell rendering. Either wrap in `` or use `next/dynamic` with `{ ssr: false }`. - **Skeleton fallbacks must be lightweight**: The fallback prop in `` renders instantly — keep it minimal (single div with spinner or shimmer classes). Heavy skeletons defeat PPR's purpose. - **`posts.json` format variance**: Some implementations have `{ posts: [...] }`, others have a flat array. The component reading it must handle both. See "JSON Data Nesting" above. - **Pitfall: Footer `new Date()` blocks PPR**: Even a single `new Date().getFullYear()` call in a server component renders the entire page as dynamic. Replace with a hardcoded year string. ## Multi-Locale Routing (Activation Pattern) All four locales (`es`, `en`, `nl`, `de`) should be activated as infrastructure, not just the default. The pattern: ### Middleware Locale Detection **CRITICAL: Cookie name must match getServerSideProps.** The middleware reads `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie, and getServerSideProps sets it as `NEXT_LOCALE`. If they disagree, Accept-Language header overrides on every visit. Also check `request.cookies.get('locale')` for backward compatibility with any previous middleware version. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. // src/middleware.ts const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es' export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { pathname } = request.nextUrl const firstSegment = pathname.split('/')[1] const hasLocale = LOCALES.includes(firstSegment) if (!hasLocale && !pathname.startsWith('/_next') && !pathname.startsWith('/images') && pathname !== '/favicon.svg') { // Read NEXT_LOCALE first (set by getServerSideProps), fall back to 'locale' (legacy) let preferred = request.cookies.get('NEXT_LOCALE')?.value || request.cookies.get('locale')?.value || DEFAULT_LOCALE if (!LOCALES.includes(preferred)) { const acceptLang = request.headers.get('accept-language') || '' for (const loc of LOCALES) { if (acceptLang.startsWith(loc) || acceptLang.includes(` ${loc}`) || acceptLang.includes(`;${loc}`)) { preferred = loc; break } } } const url = new URL(`/${preferred}${pathname}`, request.url) if (request.nextUrl.search) url.search = request.nextUrl.search return NextResponse.redirect(url, 302) } const result = NextResponse.next() return result } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|images|favicon\\.svg).*)'], } ``` ### Locale Detection in getServerSideProps — URL Prefix MUST Win **This is the most common multi-locale bug.** The URL prefix (`/es/servicios`) must ALWAYS override cookie and Accept-Language. Without tracking whether the URL explicitly specified a locale, a stale `NEXT_LOCALE=en` cookie will override `/es/`: ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params, req, res }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' let hasUrlLocale = false const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] // 1) URL prefix locale takes highest priority if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; hasUrlLocale = true slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; hasUrlLocale = true; slug = 'home' } // 2) If no URL locale, check cookie (only if URL didn't specify) if (!hasUrlLocale) { const cookieLocale = req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (cookieLocale && LOCALES.includes(cookieLocale)) { locale = cookieLocale } } // 3) If still default and no cookie, detect from Accept-Language (only if URL didn't specify) const noCookie = !req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (!hasUrlLocale && locale === 'es' && noCookie) { const detected = detectLocaleFromHeader(req?.headers?.['accept-language']) if (detected) locale = detected } // Set locale cookie for persistence const cookieMaxAge = 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', `NEXT_LOCALE=${locale}; Path=/; Max-Age=${cookieMaxAge}; SameSite=Lax`) // ... } ``` ### Header: Nav Hrefs Must Prepend Locale Prefix After stripping any multiclient prefix (`/s/en/nexa-paraguay/`), prepend the current locale to every nav href. The language switcher must also be in the header — implement a dropdown with flag emoji + language code: ```tsx // In Header.tsx const LOCALE_FLAGS = { es: '🇪🇸 ES', en: '🇬🇧 EN', nl: '🇳🇱 NL', de: '🇩🇪 DE' } const currentLocale = locale || 'es' function switchLocale(newLocale: string) { const path = router.asPath const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] let cleanPath = path const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean) if (parts.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { cleanPath = '/' + parts.slice(1).join('/') || '/' } window.location.href = '/' + newLocale + cleanPath } // Nav item href rendering let href = item.href || '#' // Strip existing multiclient prefix href = href.replace(/^\/s\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/, '') // Prepend locale prefix for internal links if (!href.startsWith('http') && !href.startsWith('/' + currentLocale)) { href = '/' + currentLocale + href } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Verification Write a comparison script (`scripts/compare-locale-keys.py`) that walks key paths across all locale JSONs using dot-separated paths and finds truly missing keys (not structural differences). Add empty strings for missing keys: ```python import json def walk(obj, path=''): results = set() if isinstance(obj, dict): for k, v in obj.items(): p = f'{path}.{k}' if path else k results.add(p) results |= walk(v, p) elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], dict): for i, item in enumerate(obj): results |= walk(item, path) else: results.add(path) return results ``` ### Catch-All Route [[...slug]] Instead of [slug] To support `/en/faq` style multi-segment URLs, use `[[...slug]]` NOT `[slug]`: ```bash mv src/pages/"[slug].tsx" src/pages/"[[...slug]].tsx" ``` This captures `/` → empty array, `/faq` → `['faq']`, `/en/faq` → `['en', 'faq']`. `index.tsx` CANNOT coexist — delete it and handle the home page case within `[[...slug]]`. ### Locale Extraction ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } const fullContent = loadJSON(process.cwd() + '/content', `${locale}.json`) || ... } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Before activating, audit all locale JSON files against the reference (`es.json`). Walk every key and check it exists in each locale. Add missing keys with empty strings (placeholders until translated). The gaps are typically 10-15 keys per locale: new pages not propagated, recently-added content keys, object sub-keys. ### Post-Deploy Enhancement Pass After the site is deployed and DNS resolves, run the batch enhancement checklist (see `client-site-enhancement-batch` skill) to ensure analytics, JSON-LD schema, OG metadata, WhatsApp integration, and docs files are present. **Minimum baseline for every launched site:** - [ ] Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights in layout - [ ] JSON-LD LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService schema in `` - [ ] OG title, description, image in metadata export - [ ] WhatsApp float button (critical for PY market) - [ ] .env.example file - [ ] README with setup + deploy instructions ## Verification ```bash for loc in es en nl de; do for page in "" faq sobre servicios programas contacto blog privacidad glosario; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://site.com/${loc}/${page}" done done ``` ## Asset Activation Policy Nothing gets deleted without client confirmation. Every dormant asset is an unactivated feature. Keep all `@ai-whisperers/*` packages installed (tree-shaken at build time, zero runtime cost). Keep unreferenced images on disk and in the manifest (pre-generated for future campaigns, <1MB total). #### Content JSON Integrity Check (Pre-Deploy) Before building the Docker image, validate ALL content JSON files. A corrupt JSON makes SSR return `content: {}` (empty pages) with zero errors in Docker logs: ```bash python3 -c " import json, os for f in os.listdir('content'): if f.endswith('.json'): try: json.load(open(f'content/{f}')) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'CORRUPT: {f} — {e}') exit(1) print('All content JSON valid') " ``` ### Stale .next/ directory pitfall After `git pull origin main` that changes content JSON files or source code, the existing `.next/` directory may be stale. The Docker `COPY . .` includes `.next/`, so stale build output + new content = empty SSR pages. Fix: ```bash rm -rf .next/ # Only when source/content files changed npm run build # Rebuild from scratch ``` After any content edit or page config change, validate all section content keys resolve: ```python import json, os with open('content/es.json') as f: content = json.load(f) for fname in os.listdir('nexa-pages'): cfg = json.load(open(f'nexa-pages/{fname}')) for sec in cfg.get('sections', []): ck = sec.get('content', sec['id']) resolved = content for part in ck.split('.'): if isinstance(resolved, dict) and part in resolved: resolved = resolved[part] else: resolved = None; break if resolved is None: print(f"MISSING: {fname} → {sec['id']} → {ck}") ``` ## Client Site Deep-Dive — Full Business Assessment When a client site already exists with docs (like Nexa Paraguay), don't just look at code — produce a structured assessment that covers ALL areas the business owner needs to see. ### Assessment Structure 1. **Code & Content Audit** — browse the live site + examine repo structure + read all docs 2. **Competitive Position** — price positioning, AI readiness, niche focus (use existing docs if they exist) 3. **Financial Model Review** — unit economics, funnel estimates, cost structure (which docs exist) 4. **Gap Analysis** — what's broken now (P0/P1) vs what needs client input 5. **Phased Roadmap** — organize into 6 phases by impact: (1) Fix broken pages, (2) Conversion path, (3) Trust & credibility, (4) SEO & analytics, (5) Multi-language, (6) AI differentiation ### Quick-Win Batch Sizing Always include a "quick-wins I can execute right now" section with concrete, batch-fixable items: - Fix page config `id` fields across all page JSONs - Add sitemap.xml + robots.txt - Replace fabricated stats with honest text - Fix blog image key mismatches - Remove `is_demo` flag - Add unique meta titles per page - Enable additional locale routing ### Business Model Impact Table Show the before/after for the client in financial terms they understand: | Metric | Current | With improvements | |---|---|---| | Clients/month | X | Y | | Avg ticket | $Z | $W | | Monthly revenue | $A | $B | ### Key Questions to Answer - What's the AI differentiation opportunity? (Check competitor landscape — most have zero AI) - What justifies the premium pricing? (AI layer, multilingual, faster process) - What's the head start window? (Usually 12-18 months before competitors catch up) - What are the 3-5 blog posts that would dominate zero-competition keywords? ## Pitfalls ### Content Honesty — MUST Replace Fabricated Data **CRITICAL: Factory-demo content ships by default.** Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. See `references/client-content-honesty.md` for the systematic audit checklist. Luana (Nexa Paraguay) explicitly called this out: "that's bullshit, not real, I don't want to have something that's not true on the page." This is a hard veto — ship fabrications and lose client trust permanently. ### Contact forms and admin panels For contact form wiring patterns (HubSpot, Mailchimp, rate-limiting, graceful fallback), see `references/contact-form-integration-pattern.md`. For admin panel setup with auth guard, see `references/admin-panel-setup.md`. For the full list of published vs unpublished `@ai-whisperers/*` packages, see `references/github-packages-ecosystem.md`. - Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. **: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. The `professional-services-website` skill is the closest umbrella, but this session's work is specifically about **enhancing an existing client site with an admin content editor, Supabase content overrides, and batch content/design updates** — not building a new site from scratch. This is a new class of work distinct from the scaffold-and-deploy pattern.

Let me check what other existing skills cover site enhancement:

messages.content: {"success": true, "name": "client-site-enhancement-batch", "description": "Run a standardized enhancement pass across multiple existing client sites. Adds analytics (Vercel + GA4), JSON-LD schema (LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService), OG metadata, WhatsApp integration, missing docs (.env.example, README), and verifies all improvements compile, commit, and push. Designed for the Ai-Whisperers fleet of 8+ Next.js client sites.", "tags": ["batch", "enhancement", "standardization", "seo", "analytics", "ops", "client"], "related_skills": ["seo-super-agent", "professional-services-website", "multi-tenant-saas-architecture", "cross-repo-architecture-comparison", "nexa-content-generation"], "content": "--- name: client-site-enhancement-batch description: >- Run a standardized enhancement pass across multiple existing client sites. Adds analytics (Vercel + GA4), JSON-LD schema (LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService), OG metadata, WhatsApp integration, missing docs (.env.example, README), and verifies all improvements compile, commit, and push. Designed for the Ai-Whisperers fleet of 8+ Next.js client sites. tags: - batch - enhancement - standardization - seo - analytics - ops - client related_skills: - seo-super-agent - professional-services-website - multi-tenant-saas-architecture - cross-repo-architecture-comparison - nexa-content-generation triggers: - improve all sites - batch improvements - standardize client sites - bring sites up to standard - add analytics to every site - add JSON-LD to all sites - site review pass - audit all sites - what can we improve - better all websites - mejorar todos los sitios - analyze what X has that Y does not - what does SITE have that SITE doesn't - compare sites - gap analysis --- # Client Site Enhancement Batch Run a standardized enhancement pass across ALL client sites in a single session. The goal: bring every site to a baseline level of analytics, SEO, schema, and documentation — then commit and push everything. ## Comprehensive Single-Site Audit Pattern When asked to "analyze [site] repo" for a single site (not a batch), produce a structured analysis report with these sections: 1. **Architecture** — next.js version, React, Tailwind, TypeScript, build config (standalone/bundled), Docker setup, Traefik config, number of replicas 2. **Pages** — list every route with page.tsx, note type (static/dynamic/API), mark completeness 3. **Components** — count, list key ones (header, footer, WhatsApp float, etc.) 4. **Features** — analytics, JSON-LD, OG tags, canonical URLs, sitemap, booking systems, admin panels, rate limiting 5. **Issues by severity:** | Level | Color | Meaning | |-------|-------|---------| | 🔴 CRITICAL | Must fix now | Data loss, SEO breakage, legal risk | | 🟡 IMPORTANT | Should fix soon | Performance, UX, maintainability | | 🔵 MENOR | Nice to have | Polish, convenience | Typical critical issues to scan for: - **Sitemap/robots.txt with placeholders** — `SITE_URL_PLACEHOLDER` → site not indexable - **File-based storage without Docker volume** — booking.json, .data/ → data lost on redeploy - **Name/pricing inconsistencies** — testimonio name doesn't match "about" name, outdated prices - **No per-page SEO metadata** — all pages share the same layout title/description 6. **Executive summary table** — score each area (visual/UX/booking/SEO/infra/code) out of 10 ## Cross-Site Analysis Methodology - After onboarding 3+ new builder sites (magnolia, depiflash, luis tier) - Quarterly maintenance pass - When a client mentions they aren't getting traffic (analytics gap is #1 cause) - Before showing a client the dashboard for the first time ## Cross-Site Analysis Methodology When a user says "analyze all the things [Site A] and [Site B] have that [Site C] should implement": - DO NOT delegate to subagents (needs direct file system access) - DO compare: file trees, app/ routes, lib/, components/, API routes - DO produce priority-ranked comparison table - Always check: error/loading/not-found pages, security headers, rate limiting, per-page SEO, content management, admin auth, Docker + healthcheck - Trap: don't propose blog/community features for brochure-style PYME sites. Filter what applies to the site type. **Execution order for a single-site upgrade:** 1. File tree comparison → identify gaps 2. Implement P0 (error pages, security headers, rate limiting if API routes exist) 3. Add per-page SEO metadata (PageMeta component pattern) 4. Add content override system (Supabase + es.json) if the site needs admin editing 5. Hardcoded value extraction (phone, WhatsApp, pricing → JSON → Supabase) 6. Docker + CI verification 7. Commit + push all at once with a comprehensive message ## Enhancement Checklist (Ranked by Impact) ### P0 — Analytics (Vercel + Speed Insights) Every site must have Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights. Without this, you can't show clients traffic data or diagnose issues. **Install:** ```bash npm install @vercel/analytics @vercel/speed-insights ``` For builder sites that use `@ai-whisperers/*` private packages and can't install from npm (401/ETARGET errors), copy the packages from a working site: ```bash cp -r /root/jota-ink-tattoo/node_modules/@vercel node_modules/ ``` Also update `package.json` to add the deps so they persist. **Layout integration:** Add to `app/layout.tsx` imports: ```tsx import { Analytics } from "@vercel/analytics/react" import { SpeedInsights } from "@vercel/speed-insights/next" ``` Add components near ``: ```tsx ``` ### P0 — JSON-LD Schema Add `application/ld+json` script to the layout ``. Type depends on site: | Site type | Schema `@type` | |-----------|----------------| | Beauty salon, tattoo, construction, physical store | `LocalBusiness` | | Software/consulting/professional services | `ProfessionalService` | | E-commerce | `Store` | **Pattern** (insert into `` after theme-color meta): ```tsx ` with the JSON stringified in __html. - **Content JSON access**: Sites that import content JSON (most do) should use `c.site?.title` etc. from the JSON. Sites without a JSON import (nexa, early sites) need hardcoded values. - **nexa uses `ProfessionalService`** not `LocalBusiness` — it's a consulting firm not a physical shop. Also include `email` field. - **elviajero already has Store schema**: Don't duplicate. Only add missing types. - **OG on nexa was completely missing**: Nexa's layout.tsx had no openGraph export at all. Needed full metadata rewrite including title template, description, OG image placeholder, canonical URL. ## Phase 3: Content Management (Post-Enhancement) After the enhancement pass, sites that need admin editing benefit from the **content override pattern**: ### Content Override Pattern (Supabase + es.json) **When:** Client needs to edit content (pricing, FAQ, phone, Instagram) without git. **Architecture:** - `es.json` stays as the renderable baseline - Supabase `ej_site_config` table stores overrides as a single JSONB row (`key="content_overrides"`) - React context fetches overrides at mount, deep-merges with es.json defaults - Admin endpoint writes to the same Supabase row **Changes required per site:** 1. `lib/content-provider.tsx` — context provider with fetch + merge 2. `app/api/content/route.ts` — public GET for overrides 3. `app/api/admin/content/route.ts` — admin PUT (with auth guard) 4. Update components that use hardcoded values to read from `content.*` instead 5. Seed Supabase row: `{ key: "content_overrides", value: { instagram: "...", phone: "..." } }` **See** `multi-tenant-saas-architecture` skill → `references/content-override-pattern.md` for full implementation details. ### Pitfalls - After adding ContentProvider, pages using `import raw from "@/content/es.json"` still work — they just don't see overrides. Migrate them incrementally. - The `/api/content` route uses service_role key server-side (not exposed to client). - Supabase down = es.json only. Content reverts to defaults gracefully. After the batch pass, content-driven sites (nexa, jota, pitchy tier) need a deeper second pass. This turns a "standard" site into a **production-grade** one. ### Phase 2 Checklist | # | Improvement | Where | Impact | |---|-------------|-------|--------| | 1 | **Error pages** (`error.tsx`, `not-found.tsx`, `loading.tsx`) | `src/app/` root | Professional error handling instead of raw Next.js defaults | | 2 | **Article schema on blog posts** | `blog/[slug]/page.tsx` | Each blog post eligible for rich SERP result (headline, date, author, image) | | 3 | **FaqPage schema** | Pages with FAQ section | Expandable rich accordion in Google search | | 4 | **BreadcrumbList schema** | Every page | Breadcrumb path in SERP, improves site structure signals | | 5 | **OG image** | `public/images/og-default.svg` | Social share preview (create SVG if no real image) | | 6 | **Share buttons** | Blog post template | WhatsApp, X, LinkedIn, Facebook — critical for Paraguay (WhatsApp) | | 7 | **Blog pagination + category filtering** | Blog section component | Don't dump all posts at once; allow filtering | | 8 | **Loading skeletons** | Suspense fallbacks for async routes | Smooth loading instead of blank flash | | 9 | **GSC verification tag** | Root layout metadata | Ready for Google Search Console setup | | 10 | **Rate limiting** | Contact + subscribe API routes | Prevent form spam (10 req/hour/IP bucket pattern) | ### Pattern: lib/schemas.ts Create a shared schema utility for consistent JSON-LD generation: ```tsx // src/lib/schemas.ts interface FaqItem { q?: string; pregunta?: string; question?: string; title?: string; a?: string; respuesta?: string; answer?: string; description?: string; } export function generateBreadcrumbSchema(baseUrl: string, currentUrl: string, pageName: string) { return { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "BreadcrumbList", itemListElement: [ { "@type": "ListItem", position: 1, name: "Inicio", item: baseUrl }, { "@type": "ListItem", position: 2, name: pageName, item: currentUrl }, ], } } export function generateFaqSchema(items: FaqItem[]) { return { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", mainEntity: items.map(item => ({ "@type": "Question", name: item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title || '', acceptedAnswer: { "@type": "Answer", text: item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || '' }, })), } } ``` ### Pattern: Blog Post Page with All Enhancements The blog post template should include: 1. Article JSON-LD schema in `` 2. BreadcrumbList JSON-LD 3. OG metadata with `type: 'article'`, `publishedTime`, `authors` 4. ShareButtons component after content 5. Tags display 6. Back-to-blog link 7. Suspense wrapper with loading skeleton fallback 8. Full `generateMetadata()` with `openGraph.images` See `references/blog-post-page-pattern.md` for the complete template. ### Pattern: ShareButtons Component A `'use client'` component with 4 share targets — critical for WhatsApp dominance in PY. ```tsx export function ShareButtons({ url, title }: { url: string; title: string }) { const encodedUrl = encodeURIComponent(url) const encodedTitle = encodeURIComponent(title) // WhatsApp: wa.me/?text= // X: twitter.com/intent/tweet // LinkedIn: linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite // Facebook: facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php return (...) // SVG icons + onClick → window.open } ``` ### Pattern: Blog Pagination + Category Filter Convert a static blog section to client-state-driven pagination: - `POSTS_PER_PAGE = 9` - `categories` extracted from post data (unique set) - Filter buttons for each category - `currentPage` + `totalPages` state - Previous/Next controls with page indicator For i18n: use a labels map per locale (labels for "Anterior/Siguiente/Página"). ### Pattern: Rate Limiting on API Routes IP-based in-memory buckets. Same code pattern for any route: ```tsx const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000 const RATE_LIMIT_MAX = 10 const ipBuckets = new Map() function getRateLimitInfo(ip: string) { // Returns { allowed, remaining, resetAt } // On rate limit: return 429 with Retry-After header } ``` ### OG Image (SVG Fallback) When no real OG image exists, create a minimal branded SVG: ```svg ``` ### Phase 2 Order of Execution 1. Error/not-found/loading pages (fast win) 2. Create `lib/schemas.ts` for shared JSON-LD generation 3. Update generic `[slug]/page.tsx` to inject BreadcrumbList + FaqPage schemas 4. Replace `blog/[slug]/page.tsx` with full Article + ShareButtons + Loading skeleton version 5. Replace `BlogSection` component with pagination + category filter version 6. Add OG image SVG to `public/images/` 7. Add GSC verification tag to root layout metadata 8. Add rate limiting to contact + subscribe API routes 9.
